title: Chorizo and Butter Bean Stew
subtitle: Spanish-style smoky sausage and bean braise
excerpt: Chorizo and butter bean stew is a deeply flavoured, quick one-pot dish of chorizo, butter beans, tomatoes, and smoked paprika. It comes together in about 25 minutes and is substantial enough to be a main course. Serve with crusty bread.
sourceNotes: A simplified version of Spanish-style chorizo and white bean dishes, related to the Asturian fabada and similar regional bean stews. This version uses tinned butter beans for speed and is intended as a weeknight dish rather than a long braise.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool and freeze in portions. Reheat gently on the hob, adding a splash of water.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles easily. Keeps in the fridge for 4 days.

content.text: Chorizo and butter bean stew is a quick, filling one-pot dish built around the flavour of smoked chorizo, smoked paprika, and tender white beans. The chorizo does most of the seasoning; the beans absorb the spiced tomato sauce as everything simmers. Ready in under 30 minutes.

items.prepNote: cooking chorizo, sliced or cut into chunks

content.text: Cook the chorizo in a casserole or large pan over medium heat for 4 to 5 minutes until the fat has rendered and the edges are lightly browned. Transfer to a plate, leaving the fat in the pan.
Add the onion to the chorizo fat and cook for 5 minutes until soft. Add the garlic and {{paprika-smoked}} of smoked paprika and stir for 1 minute.
Add the tinned tomatoes and {{chicken-stock}} of stock. Bring to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes.

Return the chorizo to the pan. Add the butter beans. Simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beans are heated through and the sauce has thickened. Add a splash of water if it looks too thick. Taste and season with salt, pepper, and sherry vinegar if using. Scatter with parsley.

items.cause: Chorizo and stock both add saltiness
items.fix: Don't season with salt until after the beans and chorizo have cooked through. Taste at the end and adjust, adding only as much as needed.

items.cause: Too much liquid for the amount of beans
items.fix: Mash a few of the butter beans with a spoon against the side of the pan; this thickens the sauce naturally.

content.text: Bean and sausage stews are made across Spain, from the Asturian fabada to the Madrilenian cocido. This is a faster, simplified version for a weeknight when the cold calls for something filling and easy. Serve with crusty bread and a glass of red wine.
